Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and contract workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\nDo not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.\nDOSH Occupational Safety and Health Act
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.

Include all fatality cases occurring at your workplace, regardless of whether the victim was an employee or any other person (such as contractors, visitors, or members of the public) present at the worksite. Refer to Chart 1 Deciding Whether a Case Should be Notified and Recorded on page 17. Fatalities resulting from occupational poisoning/diseases should be reported by the attending medical practitioner. DOSH Occupational Safety and Health Act
Please enter the total number of workplace accident cases resulting in fatality(ies) that were notified and reported to the Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) for the given year.
An occurrence, which arises out of or in connection with work, although not resulting in any accident or poisoning/disease, had the potential to do so.\n\nDOSH Occupational Safety and Health Act

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

Serious bodily injury includes but is not limited to: amputations, loss of sight, major fractures, severe burns, etc. regardless of whether the victim was an employee or any other person (such as contractors, visitors, or members of the public) present at the worksite. \nNADOPOD 2004, Schedule 1 and Regulation 5(1)
